content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 2.1 WARN — description.txt claims "Built-in ad blocker", "AI-powered suggestions", and "Works fully offline", but ContentView.swift only implements a plain in-memory task list with no ad-blocking, ML/AI, or offline-storage code (fastlane/metadata/en-US/description.txt:1-3 vs App/ContentView.swift:1-19).
Pierre: Apple reviewers routinely test marketing claims against actual app behavior, and unsubstantiated feature claims like "AI-powered" or "ad blocker" are a common 2.1/2.3 rejection reason (or later removal) if the reviewer can't find them in the app. Here there's no CoreML/CreateML import, no content-blocking extension, and no persistence layer visible — the app is a simple local task list held in `@State`, which won't even survive relaunch, contradicting "fully offline" in any meaningful sense. Please rewrite the description to match implemented functionality, or implement the claimed AI/ad-block/offline features before submission.
